subject: Re: The dautoalign4warp didn't generate the xf file
body:
I am sure the parallel computing toolbox was installed. And I did not know 
how to configure it. At the first time, I ran the script successfully. And 
then it seemed something wrong. I did not know how to configure it? Should 
I ask about it in matlab discussion group?

在2021年5月25日星期二 UTC+8 下午11:34:29<alist...@gmail.com> 写道：

> Hi Yuqi,
>
> If it managed to attempt to run tiltalign previously then something has 
> changed on your system since then to make the tilt-series alignment itself 
> break (that step previously ran without errors, judging from the output you 
> provided)
>
> Something about how your MATLAB is configured is making it fail to run 
> start a parallel pool with 28 workers.
>
> https://www.mathworks.com/help/parallel-computing/parallel-preferences.html
>
> Do you have the parallel computing toolbox installed?
>
> Cheers,
>
> Alister
>
> On 25 May 2021, at 15:54, yuqi zhang <8245...@qq.com> wrote:
>
> I ran the  dautoalign4warp again after  removing -UnbinnedPixelSize option 
> from the tiltalign_wrapper.sh   and there is an error: 
> *[mbparse.multicore.checkPool] Could not open pool for 28 workers *
> *parallel:cluster:PoolRunValidation*
> *Parallel pool failed to start with the following error. For more detailed 
> information, validate the profile 'local' in the Cluster Profile Manager.*
> *[mbparse.multicore.checkPool] Preparing to try*
> *[mbparse.multicore.checkPool] Dynamo tries to open a parallel pool for 28 
> workers*
> *Starting parallel pool (parpool) using the 'local' profile...*
>
> I have checked the license and the version. They have no questions.
>
> 在2021年5月22日星期六 UTC+8 下午6:38:14<alist...@gmail.com> 写道：
>
>> Hi Yuqi,
>>
>> Can you check the tiltalign.stdout file, it probably contains a message 
>> about an illegal option passed to tiltalign (-UnbinnedPixelSize) - this 
>> option was present in the later beta versions of IMOD 4.10.X, is not 
>> present in the current stable version (4.11.X) but is again present in the 
>> beta versions (4.12.X). This is (I think) because of an ongoing transition 
>> to the use of physical units in IMOD.
>>
>> The simplest solution is to upgrade to IMOD 4.12.X and rerun - 
>> alternatively you can remove the -UnbinnedPixelSize option from the 
>> tiltalign_wrapper.sh script in your autoalign_dynamo installation and rerun
>>
>>
>> https://github.com/alisterburt/autoalign_dynamo/blob/00c33d8d4cb7fe06e8c90a8c75a953115bd18484/autoalign/tiltalign_wrapper.sh#L35
>>
>> Sorry about that, remaining compatible various ever-improving softwares 
>> is tricky. It’s worth noting that checking the various output files files 
>> (like the tiltalign.stdout) and information about which versions the 
>> software was tested against (available in the README 
>> https://github.com/alisterburt/autoalign_dynamo) can be useful for 
>> solving these types of issues yourself!
>>
>> Cheers and good luck,
>>
>> Alister
>>
>> On 22 May 2021, at 11:27, yuqi zhang <8245...@qq.com> wrote:
>>
>> The dautoalign4warp didn't generate the xf file with the tutorial in *https://teamtomo.org/walkthrough/EMPIAR-10164/ 
>> <https://teamtomo.org/walkthrough/EMPIAR-10164/>*
>>
>> The generate files in folder dynamo_alignments/TS_??.mrc:
>> info/         taSolution.log    TS_54.mrc.mod     workingMarkers.dms
>> nominal.tlt  tiltalign.stdout  TS_54.mrc.rawtlt
>>
>> I did not know how to deal with that?
>>
>> --
